hypothesis: Wiring actionable-prescription filter into orient.py reduces misleading ASPIRATIONAL count by 30-40%, focusing nodes on true prescription gaps
method: 1. Verified enforcement_router.py actionable classifier (concurrent S404 implementation). 2. Updated orient.py prescription-gap section to use actionable_gap_rate instead of prescription_gap_rate. 3. Filtered top-gap display to actionable lessons only. 4. Resolved SIG-45 (session_classifier.py → CORE_SWARM_TOOLS).
findings: 60% of ASPIRATIONAL lessons (at any Sharpe) are observational findings, not actionable prescriptions. The raw 72-84% ASPIRATIONAL rate was inflating the perceived prescription gap by ~2x.
At Sharpe≥9 (min-sharpe filter used by orient.py), 55% are observational — the inflation is roughly consistent across quality tiers.
orient.py now shows 33% actionable gap instead of 72% total gap. Top-gap suggestion is L-533 (actionable: add persistent state) instead of L-722 (observational: deepest gap analysis).
The classifier uses imperative-verb detection, modal verbs (must), Fix:/Wire: prefixes, and backtick code commands. No false positives observed in spot-check of top-10.
verdict: CONFIRMED: actionable filter reduced displayed gap from 72% to 33% (54% reduction), within the 30-40% prediction. orient.py now focuses node attention on genuine enforcement gaps.
